result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—the idea that we are fundamentally disconnected from each other and the living systems we inhabit.  

I’d want the dominant human consciousness to shift from seeing life as a collection of isolated individuals, nations, and species competing for resources… to feeling, in a deep and operational way, that we are part of an interconnected whole.  

This isn’t just about empathy—it’s about restructuring systems (economic, legal, educational) so they reflect interdependence rather than exploitation. If that shift took root, many seemingly intractable problems—environmental destruction, systemic inequality, endless conflict—would begin to unravel, because their roots lie in seeing “the other” as separate, disposable, or inferior.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
